json.companyDescription: GreenFlux is een laadpuntexploitant en dienstverlener op het gebied van elektrisch vervoer. Met verschillende partners werken we er hard aan om elektrisch rijden in Nederland zo makkelijk en aangenaam mogelijk te maken. We bieden interoperabele laadpassen aan, installeren laadpunten bij particulieren en bedrijven en werken samen met de ANWB aan een landelijk dekkend laadnetwerk langs de snelweg.

json.title: Senior Software Engineer - DevOps
json.descriptionHtml:
At TOPIC, we work every day on high-tech innovations that make the world smarter, healthier, and better. We are searching for a Senior Software Engineer who takes ownership of the DevOps-related deliverables. Are you an experienced Senior Software Engineer who understands how modern tooling and CI/CD enhance the quality, speed, and scalability of software development?

“What I value most is the ownership and the opportunities to grow. Because our team works closely with customers, you experience the strengths of a family-like company combined with high-end, high-tech projects. It’s truly the best of both worlds.” Peter Barna | Software Architect @ TOPIC

What will you do as a Senior Software Engineer with practical DevOps experience?

As a Senior Software Engineer with practical DevOps experience, you design and develop high-quality software for complex embedded systems, working closely with both hardware and software. As a senior, you take ownership of DevOps deliverables, driving CI/CD improvements and automation of build, test, and deployment processes. You ensure technical quality and reliable delivery while supporting your team in adopting strong DevOps practices.

Your responsibilities as a Senior Software Engineer with practical DevOps experience
Design, implement, and verify subsystems, modules, and components within complex embedded systems
Integrate software into complex hardware and software systems
Apply Object-Oriented Programming, UML, unit testing, and modern design principle
Initiate and implement improvements in the team’s DevOps way of working
Set up, maintain, and improve CI/CD pipelines (build, test, release)
Automate build, test, and deployment processes to minimize manual work and errors (Python)
Support and guide colleagues in DevOps principles and practices (knowledge sharing and coaching)
Taking ownership towards the team’s technical quality and delivery reliability
Collaborating with developers, architects, testers, and operations to improve end‑to‑end delivery

What do we offer you?

This makes you smarter:
Strategic projects in complex high-tech environments
Collaboration with senior engineers and domain experts
Access to advanced training through our TOPIC Academy
Opportunities to contribute to and lead tech events and knowledge sessions

This makes you better:
Permanent contract
An all-in employment package between €57,000 and €76.000 (including net expense allowance, mobility package, 13th-month bonus, and holiday allowance). Final salary is based on relevant work experience and level of expertise
26 vacation days (with the option to purchase additional days), pension scheme and relocation budget (when you relocate to the Netherlands)

This keeps you healthy:
Strong work-life balance, flexible working hours, and hybrid working
Inspiring office environment with fresh fruit and an on-site fitness facility
Home office support to create an inspiring workspace at home
Regular fun, social, and sports events
What do you bring as a Senior Software Engineer with practical DevOps experience?
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in (Technical) Computer Science, Embedded Systems, or similar
Minimum 5 years of relevant industry experience as a Software Engineer in a high-tech or embedded environment, with a strong track record in designing and developing object-oriented software solutions in C++ and preferably Python
Experience with optimization, setting up or maintaining CI/CD and build environments is required
Experience with OOP, unit testing, Agile methodologies, and software design principles
Hands-on experience with Docker and Kubernetes is a strong plus
Our organization

Since 1996 we have been developing (embedded) systems that are essential for products manufactured by renowned high-tech companies around the world. Our projects involve edge to cloud solutions; from PCB design and FPGA to low level embedded software and application software for UI, Web, Mobile and cloud. We are well known for our high level of knowledge and expertise and are proud to be a Premier Embedded Computing Partner of AMD; technology leader in the field of System-on-Chips and FPGAs.

Diversity & Inclusion

At TOPIC, we believe that true innovation happens when people with different backgrounds, ideas, and perspectives come together. Every talent counts, and every voice matters. Diversity strengthens our creativity, and inclusion ensures that everyone feels welcome and valued. We build a culture where everyone has the space to contribute and be themselves.

json.companyDescription:
With a team of more than 130 engineers, we make the world a little better, smarter and healthier every day. It's literally in our DNA. With a focus on business along with a family spirit, we focus on highly qualified consultants with excellent technical skills in embedded software and digital hardware. On location or in-house from TOPIC's ultramodern office in Best. Our daily expertise, embedded in your future!

For 25 years, customers have been trusting on us for the development of embedded systems that are essential for products manufactured by renowned high-tech companies around the world. We are well known for the knowledge and expertise of our highly skilled engineers. We are able to maintain this by continuously investing in the development of our engineers: internal and external training courses with certifications, personalized meetups and knowledge sharing are part of this. Our projects involve edge to cloud solutions; from PCB design and FPGA to low level embedded software and application software for UI, Web, Mobile and cloud. 

Our Consultancy Services add expertise to a team on-site at our clients or offer them advice on a solution to an existing problem, a strategy or a process. In-house projects (from design and development to production and market introduction) are carried out by our engineers at the TOPIC head office. TOPIC also offers Farm Out projects: a unique service in which a TOPIC team of engineers is engaged for a specific development project of a customer.  

Our range of TOPIC Products consist of high-quality System-on-Modules that aim to accelerate product development and are scalable in performance and functionality.  

The quality of TOPIC's work and development processes is guaranteed and certified according to the ISO-13485, ISO/IEC-27001 and NEN-7510-1 standards. We are also proud to be a Premier Partner of Xilinx; technology leader in the field of System-on-Chips and FPGAs. 

We are still looking for new colleagues: (embedded) software engineers & designers, FPGA and hardware engineers. Would you like to explore if TOPIC is also embedded in your future? We look forward to meet with you!
json.companyBriefDescription: We make the world a little better, healthier and smarter every day.

json.title: Senior Software Engineer
json.descriptionHtml:
About the Company & Project

We are a global software company, dedicated to building world-class developer tools. Our mission is to automate routine tasks, speeding up production and allowing developers to focus on innovation and creativity.

Currently, we are developing a next-generation Live Debugger within our internal Innovation Hub. This product focuses on safe, AI-first debugging of production systems. It is designed to help engineering teams investigate issues in real-world environments with a focus on safety, security, and low overhead. Technically, the core of the product is a sophisticated mix of JVM tooling, program analysis, and AI integration.

About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer with strong enterprise JVM experience. This role is ideal for a proactive engineer excited by complex challenges at the intersection of production systems, developer tools, and AI agents.

In this role, you will:

  • Design, build, and lead the development of product features.
  • Develop tooling and automation for AI-first engineering workflows.
  • Work on enterprise integrations and deployment scenarios across Kubernetes and cloud environments.
  • Improve reliability, testing, performance, and security for production use.

We’d like to have you on the team if you:

  • Have extensive experience with Java, Kotlin, and JVM systems.
  • Have worked on enterprise backend software, infrastructure, or developer tooling.
  • Are comfortable with Kubernetes and cloud environments.
  • Take ownership, communicate clearly, and move things forward without waiting for perfect specs.
  • Enjoy using AI in development while validating results with solid engineering discipline.

Bonus points if you:

  • Have experience with debugging, instrumentation, or observability-related tools.
  • Have experience with .NET or Python backend products.
  • Care deeply about developer productivity and engineering quality.

What we offer:

  • Competitive Compensation: A strong base salary reflecting your skills and experience.
  • Work Flexibility: Flexible work location (home or office) and a "work from abroad" policy (up to 30 days per year).
  • Time Off: Extra vacation days to relax and recharge.
  • Health & Wellbeing: Medical insurance allowance, mental health support, and sports benefits (on-site gym or stipend).
  • Growth: Access to conferences, professional courses, and language classes.
  • Relocation & Logistics: Relocation support, lunch allowance, and regular internal team events.

Have experience with .NET or Python backend products.
Care deeply about developer productivity and engineering quality.
What we offer:
Competitive Compensation: A strong base salary reflecting your skills and experience.
Work Flexibility: Flexible work location (home or office) and a "work from abroad" policy (up to 30 days per year).
Time Off: Extra vacation days to relax and recharge.
Health & Wellbeing: Medical insurance allowance, mental health support, and sports benefits (on-site gym or stipend).
Growth: Access to conferences, professional courses, and language classes.
Relocation & Logistics: Relocation support, lunch allowance, and regular internal team events.

json.title: Senior Tools Engineer
json.descriptionHtml:
Frankfurt Programming / Engineering CRYENGINE

We are looking for an experienced and talented Senior Tools Engineer for our CRYENGINE Tools team at our new state-of-the-art office in Frankfurt, Germany.

As a Senior Tools Engineer, you will be working in a cooperative, cross-disciplinary environment to develop industry-leading tools, contributing to the improvement of productivity and quality for game teams and the CRYENGINE Sandbox Editor. As part of our innovative and ambitious team, you can expect your abilities and scope of duties to grow as you make a real difference.


What You'll Do
    • Help create and deploy our suite of development tools, both in-cloud and on-prem, across multiple platforms.
    • Contribute to the development of the CRYENGINE Sandbox Editor.
    • Collaborate closely with artists, designers, and software engineers on the requirements and design of tools.
    • Propose tools and methods to increase the efficiency and quality of content development.
    • Contribute to maintenance and improvements of critical low-level systems.
    • Ensure reliability and stability of the code base, and drive adoption of new techniques, technologies and languages.
What You Will Deliver
    • 5+ years of relevant professional software development experience.
    • Excellent C#, C++ programming skills.
    • Experience with distributed, micro-service architectures and software configuration and deployment with Kubernetes.
    • Experience developing user interfaces and working with HTML and Qt frameworks (or similar).
    • Expertise with performance profiling tools.
    • Willing to relocate to Frankfurt am Main area, Germany.
Nice To Have (Not Required)
    • Experience working with multithreaded, real-time systems.
    • Knowledge of WPF and/or TSX (e.g. React)
    • Strong TypeScript, Python, Rust, Go programming skills.
    • Experience with any game Engines preferably experience with CRYENGINE.

What you can expect from us

Career Path
Your professional development is important to us, so we have laid out a career plan to help you progress towards your goals and objectives.
Relocation Support
We offer a relocation budget and full coverage of flights to Frankfurt for you and your family.
You can expect extensive assistance with visa, work permits, and communication with authorities during the relocation process, as well as help settling into Germany (e.g. setting up appointments with banks, government agencies, schools, landlords, finding apartments etc.).
Company Apartment
To help you get settled, we provide you with a fully furnished company apartment during your first three months in Frankfurt.
Public Transport Pass
Discover Frankfurt by bus, tram and metro – free of charge.
Gym Card
A healthy body is a healthy mind. We offer a membership at the premium gym chain Fitness First in Germany. Work out, join group fitness classes, or relax in the wellness facilities.
State-of-the-art Office
We’ve recently moved into a brand-new, modern office located in the heart of Frankfurt. Our new workspace is designed to inspire creativity and collaboration, with open areas, quiet zones, and top-tier facilities — all just steps away from public transport, restaurants, bars and cultural hotspots.
Hybrid Working
We believe in the value of in-person collaboration while also supporting flexibility. Our hybrid working model includes three days per week in the office and the option to work remotely up to two days per week, unless role-specific requirements or business needs require you to be on site.
Equal Opportunity & Inclusion
We’re building a workplace where everyone belongs. We welcome people of all backgrounds and identities and are proud to be a truly global team, with employees from over 42 countries contributing their ideas, cultures, and perspectives every day.
German Classes
Understanding the local culture will make your stay abroad more enjoyable, and Crytek supports that by offering German language courses for you and your family.
Events
Join us on our exciting company events, including new starter breakfasts, summer and winter parties, our annual trip to Gamescom in Cologne, and many more!
Vacation Days
At our Frankfurt office you can enjoy 24 days of vacation per year, and every 2 years you get 1 more (up to a maximum of 28 days). You will also have on average 10 public holidays on top of the days you take off.

Please apply directly online and, if applicable, upload your materials as specified on the job posting.

json.companyDescription: Crytek is a German video game company, founded in 1999 by brothers Cevat, Avni, and Faruk Yerli, that is headquartered in Frankfurt. Crytek has six other studios, in Budapest, Istanbul, Kiev, Seoul, Shanghai, and Sofia.The company is best known for developing Far Cry and the Crysis series, the open world nature of their games which showcase the company's CryEngine, and for pushing the limits on PC specifications to achieve advanced graphics and gameplay.

json.title: Senior Software Engineer - Credit Risk Domain
json.descriptionHtml:
It's no wonder we can go further, faster together.

We're a team of 450+ professionals from 50+ nationalities, all commercially driven, entrepreneurial and ready to turn potential into progress. Not just for us, but for the world too.

At STX, we're focused on so much more than making money. We're here to make a profit with a purpose. Because for us, nothing's more important than being able to trade in environmental commodities and create the most effective decarbonization strategies for our clients. Join us and together we can change the world – one trade at a time.

Your success story starts here…

ABOUT THE TEAM
You will be part of the Digital Engineering domain that owns the Climate Tech and Originate platforms, which power all supply and demand side touchpoints at STX.
Our domain's mission is to make every counterparty interaction fully self serve and delightful by removing operational burden and unlocking the next phase of STX's growth. You will be responsible for building and scaling the system that power credit risk assessment, monitoring, and decision-making at STX. We design and deliver systems where complex workflows feel simple, intuitive, and reliable; so counterparties experience STX as the trusted default for trading and originating with.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Design and develop scalable services supporting the full trading lifecycle, from counterparty onboarding and risk assessment to exposure monitoring, limit management, and reportingfront-office origination to back-office settlement and reconciliation.
  • Create clean APIs, modular architectures, and data pipelines to ensure clarity, performance, and reliability across the digital platform.
  • Translate complex business processes into maintainable, production-ready software that scales with organizational needs.
  • Contribute to technical excellence by writing high-quality, well-tested code and participating in design/code reviews to raise team standards.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with Trading, Risk, Finance, Operations, and Data teams to deliver robust, well-architected solutions.
  • Mentor and grow engineering talent, sharing best practices, modeling craftsmanship, and fostering a culture of clarity and accountability.

KEY REQUIREMENTS

  • 6-8+ years of experience building scalable, reliable software, ideally in fintech, trading platforms, or data-intensive systems.
  • Prior experience working within credit risk or financial risk systems is strongly preferred.
  • Proficiency in TypeScript with strong backend experience in Python or Node.js, and hands-on experience with AWS, serverless, and event-driven architectures.
  • Practical experience with infrastructure-as-code tools (CDK/Terraform), Kubernetes, and cloud-native design patterns.
  • Strong system design and engineering fundamentals, including clean architecture, testing, and production-grade software development.
  • Excellent collaboration and communication skills, able to bridge technical and business perspectives and give/receive constructive feedback.
  • Curiosity and growth mindset, staying up-to-date with emerging technologies like LLM-powered automation, serverless systems, and data-centric design.

COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS

  • Competitive remuneration package
  • Pension scheme contributions
  • Strong focus on personal and professional development
  • Sport and training opportunities
  • Assistance with relocation to the respective office location

DIVERSITY STATEMENT

We encourage and welcome applications from candidates of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives to enhance our team and contribute to a more inclusive work environment. Diversity, inclusion, and belonging are more than just concepts. They are part of our everyday work culture, ensuring employees feel welcomed and build meaningful connections. At STX, we ask applicants to think holistically about roles. If the roles sounds exciting and a great fit for you, but some of the criteria or required experience doesn't 100% line up, we still encourage you to apply!

GET IN TOUCH

Are you ready to embark on a journey with us? Please submit your application for this job by clicking on the "apply" button. For further questions, please contact the Recruitment department at recruitment@stxgroup.com.

STX Group offers equal employment opportunity. Acquisition based on this vacancy is not appreciated.

json.companyDescription: We trade environmental commodities that drive both profit and impact. As the leading global environmental commodity trader and climate solutions provider, we are building a business fit for the future, where financial growth and environmental responsibility go hand in hand. We identify opportunities early and act decisively in global markets. That’s why we are looking for entrepreneurial minds who are driven by profit and purpose.

json.title: Senior Software Engineer – Mid-Office
json.descriptionHtml:
About BlockTech

BlockTech is a fast-paced algorithmic trading firm facilitating global cryptocurrency derivatives and spot trading while expanding into new markets. As we continue to grow rapidly, we are looking for a Senior Software Engineer to take technical ownership of our Mid-Office systems and help shape how they evolve through our scale-up phase.

This is a role for an engineer who has already built and operated correctness-critical systems at scale and who wants their technical decisions to set direction, not just follow it.

What will you do?

You will own the systems that keep our trading operation accurate at every level — positions, PnL, instruments, hedging, and the data flowing between them. Across exchanges and asset classes, your work determines whether the numbers our traders and risk team rely on are correct. As a senior engineer, you will also set the technical standards the rest of the team builds against.

You will:

  • Own the books. Maintain and evolve our position, trade, and PnL systems so they remain accurate across every venue.
  • Architect the platform that supports trading. Design services that handle real-time exchange flow with the throughput and reliability our quoting and hedging depend on, making the structural calls that keep them fast and correct under load.
  • Own the data flows. Operate and harden the public and private pipelines bringing market prices, account state, and trade events into our systems in real time.
  • Lead venue integrations end-to-end. Deliver exchange and prime-broker integrations across protocol, account model, settlement, reconciliation, and surveillance and define the patterns we reuse for the next one.
  • Model the instrument universe. Own the systems describing every tradable instrument - lifecycle, expiry, margin, RFQs, fast-path quoting - where both correctness and latency matter.
  • Build self-healing systems. Design the detection and repair flows that identify position discrepancies and correct them automatically.
  • Own hedging infrastructure. Design and refine the gamma and delta hedging systems our trading strategies depend on.
  • Deliver reporting. Provide the data internal stakeholders and regulators require, like PnL breakdowns, trade surveillance, treasury accounting, and compliance.
  • Raise the bar. Mentor junior engineers, lead by example in code and design reviews, and influence the technical direction of the wider team.

What's in it for you?

  • A full-time position in a relaxed and modern office in Amsterdam
  • Competitive compensation consisting of a base salary combined with a very attractive bonus plan based on individual and company performance
  • Outstanding performance is rewarded with the opportunity to buy into the trading fund
  • Pension plan, company laptop, and reimbursement of your internet costs at home
  • An extensive in-house training program and an annual learning & development budget
  • The opportunity to work at the forefront of automated trading using state-of-the-art technology, in an environment that embraces AI to both accelerate productivity and advance our systems and models
  • Gym membership reimbursement and the opportunity to engage in various sports during working hours, including kickboxing, CrossFit, fitness, and soccer
  • Regular social events, including weekly Friday drinks, monthly outings, quarterly sports competitions and bi-annual trips abroad
  • Daily breakfast and warm lunch. Additionally, snacks and drinks are provided throughout the day
  • For internationals: All-in relocation package and Dutch classes

Our culture

At our core, we are a team of passionate trading and tech enthusiasts committed to revolutionizing trading through automation. Our collaborative approach ensures that everyone contributes to achieving our ambitious goals.

In this role, you will be both challenged and stretched, with ample opportunities for growth and development, both within and beyond your main responsibilities.

We invest in our people by offering competitive compensation and benefits, fostering innovation, and celebrating success. Our commitment to personal development transcends traditional norms; we provide an equal playing field, promote reverse leadership, and offer boundless growth opportunities for all our employees.

Join us to unlock your potential and drive innovation in the exciting world of algorithmic trading.


  • 5+ years of commercial software engineering experience, including a track record of designing and operating production systems where correctness and reliability are non-negotiable.
  • Strong proficiency across our stack. You are comfortable moving between a Rust service, a Python data pipeline, and a complex production issue, and choosing the right tool for each.
  • Demonstrated experience building low-latency, high-throughput, or distributed systems; experience in trading, fintech, or another correctness-critical domain is a strong plus.
  • Experience with Grafana & PostgreSQL.
  • A critical mind that follows things until completion, and the judgment to know what "done" means.
  • The ability to confidently design and own completely new components from first principles.
  • A high level of responsibility and ownership, and the instinct to lift the people around you.
  • Good written and verbal communication skills in English.

Location: Amsterdam

Department: Tech

Short paragraph: As a Software Engineer in our Mid-office team, you’ll design, build, and maintain high-performance systems that power global crypto trading. From scalable data solutions to real-time monitoring and compliance reporting, you’ll own projects end-to-end, collaborating closely with trading and operations to drive efficiency, accuracy, and innovation.

json.title: Senior Software Engineer
json.descriptionHtml:
At Payconiq we provide a complete Payment Platform for Banks, Merchants and Payment Service providers. Our 150-person team from more than 40 different backgrounds is continuing to modernise existing payment infrastructure with proven, scalable, real-time payment solutions across the entire omnichannel experience.

We at Payconiq live collaboration, transparency, ownership, adaptability to change and achieving excellence. All of us are constantly on the lookout to raise the bar even higher than yesterday. Together we make sure we’re ever-evolving!


To help us achieve our never-ending goal to learn and grow we are now looking for an experienced Senior Software Engineer to join our international team of open-minded, ambitious and fun colleagues. Do you like what we do and what we stand for? Do you want to grow with us and add real value? Apply now and find out what we have to offer you!

Being a Senior Software Engineer at Payconiq means you will contribute to the whole backend software development lifecycle. You will take the lead in determining the software requirements, creating the architecture and system design, writing the code, testing and deployment. While you are bound to one of our feature areas, you will work within a cross-functional team and support other teams where necessary. Your team will be working on different applications within our payment platform such as iDeal. On a day-to-day basis your focus will be on writing the cleanest code while taking existing system designs into consideration and mentoring others. In addition, you will contribute to and take a key role in further improving and building out our software architecture. You are always on the lookout for ways to do things even better and inspire to share your findings with others.


As a Senior Software Engineer you’re able to bring:

  • The desire to take ownership over everything you do.
  • A self-starter mentality, being able to take the lead on backend software projects and architectural designs.
  • A critical view; always looking for ways to further improve your own and our teams efforts.
  • A desire to work with cutting-edge tech.
  • Good communication skills; high proficiency in English and the ability to clearly and concretely express yourself.
  • An analytical mindset with an intrinsic sense of curiosity; being able to see the bigger picture and wanting to understand how things are working at all levels of the system.
  • Positivity and a ‘can-do’ attitude; always looking for ways to create a welcoming environment for colleagues and never shy to take action where needed.
  • Your honesty opinion and an ability to provide constructive feedback to others.
  • Responsibility for our code quality and system designs.
  • A desire to share knowledge and the ability to act as a mentor to others.
  • The ambition to grow with us and have fun while doing so.


In addition, you’re able to bring the following technical expertise:

  • 7+ years of experience as a Backend Software Developer, having worked on various projects and experience working with various coding styles and languages.
  • Experience of software architecture and system design.
  • Ability to write high quality modular, scalable and reusable code; you live TDD & BDD.
  • Extensive knowledge of the backend software development lifecycle.
  • Understanding of Kotlin (considered as an advantage).
  • Solid understanding of Microservices, REST APIs and Event sourcing.
  • Deep understanding of Java, Spring Frameworks, MongoDB and NoSQL.
  • Decent understanding of and experience with CI/CD, AWS, Kubernetes and Docker.
  • Experience working with agile methodologies and best practices.


If you like what you’ve read, feel free to apply and get the conversation started.


Not sold just yet? Have a peek at some of our perks:

  • Salary of EUR 65.000 – 75.000 gross per year
  • 30 vacation days per year with the possibility to purchase up to 10 extra days
  • Commuting allowance covers 0.21€ per km
  • Pension plan (in addition to your base salary, no personal contribution, fully paid by employer)
  • Collective health insurance with CZ (reduced fees)
  • ANW, WIA, collective accident insurance (paid by Payconiq)
  • Training Budget: 5% of annual gross salary (in addition to your base salary)
  • WFH allowance: EUR 2 per worked day from home
  • Internet allowance: 25 euro per month
  • Desk & Chair from employer: employee is owner
  • Mobile phone policy: bring your own device EUR 500 per 2-year, SIM card from Payconiq
  • Structural WFH Policy: hybrid model
  • Voluntary work: 3 days per year
  • MacBook pro laptop
  • LinkedIn Learning
  • ASR Vitality program
  • Relocation package: Visa sponsorship and digital support throughout the relocation process


How the hiring process will look like:

  • 3 interviews with: HR, your team and the Hiring Manager

Do you want to grow with us and add real value? Apply now and find out what we have to offer!

On a day-to-day basis your focus will be on writing the cleanest code while taking existing system designs into consideration and mentoring others. In addition, you will contribute to and take a key role in further improving and building out our software architecture. You are always on the lookout for ways to do things even better and inspire to share your findings with others.

As a Senior Software Engineer you’re able to bring:

The desire to take ownership over everything you do.
A self-starter mentality, being able to take the lead on backend software projects and architectural designs.
A critical view; always looking for ways to further improve your own and our teams efforts.
A desire to work with cutting-edge tech.
Good communication skills; high proficiency in English and the ability to clearly and concretely express yourself.
An analytical mindset with an intrinsic sense of curiosity; being able to see the bigger picture and wanting to understand how things are working at all levels of the system.
Positivity and a ‘can-do’ attitude; always looking for ways to create a welcoming environment for colleagues and never shy to take action where needed.
Your honesty opinion and an ability to provide constructive feedback to others.
Responsibility for our code quality and system designs.
A desire to share knowledge and the ability to act as a mentor to others.
The ambition to grow with us and have fun while doing so.

In addition, you’re able to bring the following technical expertise:

7+ years of experience as a Backend Software Developer, having worked on various projects and experience working with various coding styles and languages.
Experience of software architecture and system design.
Ability to write high quality modular, scalable and reusable code; you live TDD & BDD.
Extensive knowledge of the backend software development lifecycle.
Understanding of Kotlin (considered as an advantage).
Solid understanding of Microservices, REST APIs and Event sourcing.
Deep understanding of Java, Spring Frameworks, MongoDB and NoSQL.
Decent understanding of and experience with CI/CD, AWS, Kubernetes and Docker.
Experience working with agile methodologies and best practices.

If you like what you’ve read, feel free to apply and get the conversation started.

Not sold just yet? Have a peek at some of our perks:

Salary of EUR 65.000 – 75.000 gross per year
30 vacation days per year with the possibility to purchase up to 10 extra days
Commuting allowance covers 0.21€ per km
Pension plan (in addition to your base salary, no personal contribution, fully paid by employer)
Collective health insurance with CZ (reduced fees)
ANW, WIA, collective accident insurance (paid by Payconiq)
Training Budget: 5% of annual gross salary (in addition to your base salary)
WFH allowance: EUR 2 per worked day from home
Internet allowance: 25 euro per month
Desk & Chair from employer: employee is owner
Mobile phone policy: bring your own device EUR 500 per 2-year, SIM card from Payconiq
Structural WFH Policy: hybrid model
Voluntary work: 3 days per year
MacBook pro laptop
LinkedIn Learning
ASR Vitality program
Relocation package: Visa sponsorship and digital support throughout the relocation process

How the hiring process will look like:

3 interviews with: HR, your team and the Hiring Manager

Do you want to grow with us and add real value? Apply now and find out what we have to offer!

-

json.title: Senior Software Engineer - DevOps
json.descriptionHtml:
At TOPIC, we work every day on high-tech innovations that make the world smarter, healthier, and better. We are searching for a Senior Software Engineer who takes ownership of the DevOps-related deliverables. Are you an experienced Senior Software Engineer who understands how modern tooling and CI/CD enhance the quality, speed, and scalability of software development?

“What I value most is the ownership and the opportunities to grow. Because our team works closely with customers, you experience the strengths of a family-like company combined with high-end, high-tech projects. It’s truly the best of both worlds.” Peter Barna | Software Architect @ TOPIC

What will you do as a Senior Software Engineer with practical DevOps experience?

As a Senior Software Engineer with practical DevOps experience, you design and develop high-quality software for complex embedded systems, working closely with both hardware and software. As a senior, you take ownership of DevOps deliverables, driving CI/CD improvements and automation of build, test, and deployment processes. You ensure technical quality and reliable delivery while supporting your team in adopting strong DevOps practices.

Your responsibilities as a Senior Software Engineer with practical DevOps experience
Design, implement, and verify subsystems, modules, and components within complex embedded systems
Integrate software into complex hardware and software systems
Apply Object-Oriented Programming, UML, unit testing, and modern design principle
Initiate and implement improvements in the team’s DevOps way of working
Set up, maintain, and improve CI/CD pipelines (build, test, release)
Automate build, test, and deployment processes to minimize manual work and errors (Python)
Support and guide colleagues in DevOps principles and practices (knowledge sharing and coaching)
Taking ownership towards the team’s technical quality and delivery reliability
Collaborating with developers, architects, testers, and operations to improve end‑to‑end delivery

What do we offer you?

This makes you smarter:
Strategic projects in complex high-tech environments
Collaboration with senior engineers and domain experts
Access to advanced training through our TOPIC Academy
Opportunities to contribute to and lead tech events and knowledge sessions

This makes you better:
Permanent contract
An all-in employment package between €57,000 and €76.000 (including net expense allowance, mobility package, 13th-month bonus, and holiday allowance). Final salary is based on relevant work experience and level of expertise
26 vacation days (with the option to purchase additional days), pension scheme and relocation budget (when you relocate to the Netherlands)

This keeps you healthy:
Strong work-life balance, flexible working hours, and hybrid working
Inspiring office environment with fresh fruit and an on-site fitness facility
Home office support to create an inspiring workspace at home
Regular fun, social, and sports events
What do you bring as a Senior Software Engineer with practical DevOps experience?
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in (Technical) Computer Science, Embedded Systems, or similar
Minimum 5 years of relevant industry experience as a Software Engineer in a high-tech or embedded environment, with a strong track record in designing and developing object-oriented software solutions in C++ and preferably Python
Experience with optimization, setting up or maintaining CI/CD and build environments is required
Experience with OOP, unit testing, Agile methodologies, and software design principles
Hands-on experience with Docker and Kubernetes is a strong plus
Our organization

Since 1996 we have been developing (embedded) systems that are essential for products manufactured by renowned high-tech companies around the world. Our projects involve edge to cloud solutions; from PCB design and FPGA to low level embedded software and application software for UI, Web, Mobile and cloud. We are well known for our high level of knowledge and expertise and are proud to be a Premier Embedded Computing Partner of AMD; technology leader in the field of System-on-Chips and FPGAs.

Diversity & Inclusion

At TOPIC, we believe that true innovation happens when people with different backgrounds, ideas, and perspectives come together. Every talent counts, and every voice matters. Diversity strengthens our creativity, and inclusion ensures that everyone feels welcome and valued. We build a culture where everyone has the space to contribute and be themselves.

json.companyDescription:
With a team of more than 130 engineers, we make the world a little better, smarter and healthier every day. It's literally in our DNA. With a focus on business along with a family spirit, we focus on highly qualified consultants with excellent technical skills in embedded software and digital hardware. On location or in-house from TOPIC's ultramodern office in Best. Our daily expertise, embedded in your future!

For 25 years, customers have been trusting on us for the development of embedded systems that are essential for products manufactured by renowned high-tech companies around the world. We are well known for the knowledge and expertise of our highly skilled engineers. We are able to maintain this by continuously investing in the development of our engineers: internal and external training courses with certifications, personalized meetups and knowledge sharing are part of this. Our projects involve edge to cloud solutions; from PCB design and FPGA to low level embedded software and application software for UI, Web, Mobile and cloud. 

Our Consultancy Services add expertise to a team on-site at our clients or offer them advice on a solution to an existing problem, a strategy or a process. In-house projects (from design and development to production and market introduction) are carried out by our engineers at the TOPIC head office. TOPIC also offers Farm Out projects: a unique service in which a TOPIC team of engineers is engaged for a specific development project of a customer.  

Our range of TOPIC Products consist of high-quality System-on-Modules that aim to accelerate product development and are scalable in performance and functionality.  

The quality of TOPIC's work and development processes is guaranteed and certified according to the ISO-13485, ISO/IEC-27001 and NEN-7510-1 standards. We are also proud to be a Premier Partner of Xilinx; technology leader in the field of System-on-Chips and FPGAs. 

We are still looking for new colleagues: (embedded) software engineers & designers, FPGA and hardware engineers. Would you like to explore if TOPIC is also embedded in your future? We look forward to meet with you!
json.companyBriefDescription: We make the world a little better, healthier and smarter every day.

json.title: (Senior) Java Software Engineer
json.descriptionHtml:
Company Description


You’re thinking about moving abroad to take your career to the next level? WAES has several awesome opportunities to work at top notch tech companies in the Netherlands. The only thing you have to do is pack your bags, we take care of everything else.

WAES is a new school Software consultancy company in the Netherlands, specialized in large-scale cloud development. Our mission is to build a strong team of Software Developers and Data Engineers from all over the world who can solve our clients' toughest challenges. Our clients are the absolute A players in the Dutch tech environment, mainly operating in High-Tech, Fintech, Healthcare and E-Commerce.

We love to push technology further to change people's lives for the better. Let’s shape our future.


Job Description


WAES is looking for a (Senior) Java Software Engineer to join our team of software and data engineers in the Netherlands.

As a (Senior) Java Software Engineer at WAES, you are building and optimizing cool components of backend solutions for multinational organizations in the Netherlands. Whether it is transforming monolithic software to a scalable microservices environment or building new functionalities. Working on-site (when possible), you build solutions of strategic importance.

As the WAES (Senior) Java Software Engineer you:

  • Work on long-term projects at highly dynamic and fast-paced WAES clients in the Netherlands;
  • Become part of an engineering team, always at the heart of the company’s IT departments;
  • Create microservices that run within ecosystems on AWS, GCP, and/or Azure cloud;
  • Deliver new features and functionalities of big importance to the company’s core business;
  • Have ownership of your own development to become the best you can be.

Qualifications


The ideal candidate has:

  • Minimum of 3-5 years hands-on professional experience writing clean and maintainable code in Java 8+;
  • Experience with the Spring ecosystem;
  • Experience with Microservices, REST services;
  • Worked on SQL or NoSQL databases;
  • Experience writing and performing unit tests and integration tests;
  • Solid hands-on experience in cloud-related projects using AWS, Google GCP, and/or Azure;
  • Experience working in cross-functional teams according to Agile methodologies;
  • Advanced level of English (spoken and written).

Additional Information


The benefits from working at WAES:

  • You get a % of the profit, on top of your salary.
  • You receive an annual budget to attend conferences and further develop your skills.
  • Our relocation team assists you in all ways possible when moving to the Netherlands.
  • We sponsor your relocation to the Netherlands.
  • You get to work on challenging projects for top-notch tech companies in the Netherlands.
  • We encourage you to speak at in- and external Tech Talks.
  • Our unique Learning & Development program helps you advance in your career.
  • You’ll be part of our warm community of expats.
  • You get to attend the awesome WAES events.
  • We get together regularly for parties, BBQs, FIFA tournaments, hackathons, lunches, etc.

At WAES, you are welcome for who you are. No matter where you’re from and what you look like. Our community is for everyone. We strive to create an inclusive environment, empower employees and embrace diversity. We encourage everyone to respond and want to thank you for considering WAES to be your future employer.

Employment agencies should not respond to this vacancy.

json.title: Senior Software Engineer
json.descriptionHtml:
About the Company & Project

We are a global software company, dedicated to building world-class developer tools. Our mission is to automate routine tasks, speeding up production and allowing developers to focus on innovation and creativity.

Currently, we are developing a next-generation Live Debugger within our internal Innovation Hub. This product focuses on safe, AI-first debugging of production systems. It is designed to help engineering teams investigate issues in real-world environments with a focus on safety, security, and low overhead. Technically, the core of the product is a sophisticated mix of JVM tooling, program analysis, and AI integration.

About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer with strong enterprise JVM experience. This role is ideal for a proactive engineer excited by complex challenges at the intersection of production systems, developer tools, and AI agents.

In this role, you will:

  • Design, build, and lead the development of product features.
  • Develop tooling and automation for AI-first engineering workflows.
  • Work on enterprise integrations and deployment scenarios across Kubernetes and cloud environments.
  • Improve reliability, testing, performance, and security for production use.

We’d like to have you on the team if you:

  • Have extensive experience with Java, Kotlin, and JVM systems.
  • Have worked on enterprise backend software, infrastructure, or developer tooling.
  • Are comfortable with Kubernetes and cloud environments.
  • Take ownership, communicate clearly, and move things forward without waiting for perfect specs.
  • Enjoy using AI in development while validating results with solid engineering discipline.

Bonus points if you:

  • Have experience with debugging, instrumentation, or observability-related tools.
  • Have experience with .NET or Python backend products.
  • Care deeply about developer productivity and engineering quality.

What we offer:

  • Competitive Compensation: A strong base salary reflecting your skills and experience.
  • Work Flexibility: Flexible work location (home or office) and a "work from abroad" policy (up to 30 days per year).
  • Time Off: Extra vacation days to relax and recharge.
  • Health & Wellbeing: Medical insurance allowance, mental health support, and sports benefits (on-site gym or stipend).
  • Growth: Access to conferences, professional courses, and language classes.
  • Relocation & Logistics: Relocation support, lunch allowance, and regular internal team events.

json.title: Software Engineer - Store
json.descriptionHtml:
In a nutshell

At Picnic, we're redefining how people buy groceries by making shopping effortless, sustainable, and fun! Our Consumer domain is at the heart of this transformation, creating customer-facing features that delight millions of users across Europe.

From developing personalized shopping experiences, like our Meal Planner, to building engaging loyalty programs, we focus on delivering a high-quality, intuitive user experience by building scalable solutions with the latest tech. If you're passionate about building impactful products used by millions daily, this is the place for you!

Tricks of the trade

  • Ownership: Take ownership of projects from concept to production. Work collaboratively, grow alongside your team, and contribute to the team's success.
  • Impact: Develop solutions that make shopping smarter, faster, and more intuitive. Your work will power seamless, scalable, and personalized interactions for millions of users.
  • Agility: Thrive in a fast-paced environment where you learn and use a wide variety of tools and platforms to build the best tech for our customers.
  • Platform thinking: Build technology that empowers developers and business analysts alike to develop flexible features. (Check out our blog post!)
  • Our Tech: You'll work with Java and TypeScript on a daily basis, using frameworks like Spring (Spring with Spring Boot, Reactor, Immutables) and React. Supporting this core stack are GraalVM, Apache Calcite, Maven, Python 3.x, MongoDB, PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, Kafka, Git, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Helm, Terraform, Vault, and Datadog.

We're looking for

  • You have a Master's Degree (in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Information Technology, Computer Engineering or a related technical field) or equivalent experience.
  • You have 2-5 years of professional experience in Java.
  • You have a product mindset and treat technology as a means to an end, owning and solving business problems by choosing the best-fitting technical solutions and tools for each challenge.
  • You have experience with AI tooling and are comfortable with agentic engineering.
  • Your English skills are on point (no Dutch required).

Picnic Perks

✍ Every expert was once a beginner!

You'll get plenty of opportunities to challenge yourself and grow, including the Picnic Tech Academy, Lunch & Learn sessions, and tailored soft skills training. We also offer free professional weekly language courses.


Make a difference

You'll work in an awesome startup environment with the freedom to drive your own projects and create a visible impact. Our fully electric vehicles and sustainable business model mean you'll also be contributing to making the world a better place!


‍ Teamwork makes the dream work

With more than 80 nationalities across 3 countries, you'll be part of a diverse company with plenty of cool stuff to get involved with, from board game evenings to after-work drinks to our company ski trip and more!


You are what you eat

You'll get freshly prepared, healthy lunches and snacks (with plenty of vegetarian, vegan, and halal options). Coffee snob? Don't worry, our amazing Picnic barista has you covered.


Stay healthy

Mental health is important. As well as having the option to speak with Picnic colleagues who act as confidential advisors, our collaboration with OpenUp gives you easy access to professional psychologists, along with workshops and materials. There are plenty of sports communities and events to get involved with, from tennis to yoga, to climbing!


Attractive package

We offer competitive compensation and a pension plan that looks out for your future self, as well as 25 vacation days per year, so you can recharge your batteries


Benefits for expats

It can be daunting starting a new job AND moving to a new country. That's why we offer lots of support for our many expat colleagues, if you want to find our relocation benefits, see here.

json.companyDescription:
At Picnic, we're transforming grocery shopping without physical stores, making it cheaper, easier, faster, and more sustainable since 2015. With +160 locations across 3 countries, we grow thanks to our passionate people and our iconic electric cars!

Personal and professional growth are our priority. We offer our colleagues individual mentorship with assigned team leads, custom-made development paths on our internal learning platform, language courses, and career opportunities through our internal vacancies website.

Want to join one of our warehouses? You’re welcome without a CV nor previous experience! We offer you free lunch and dinner every day, and a multicultural environment with more than 80 nationalities!
Interested in becoming a delivery driver? You’ll explore your city in your own electric vehicle! We also offer you lots of free snacks and a ping pong table to enjoy your break. On top of that, we encourage all our colleagues to join our legendary Picnic parties!

Are you looking for a side job to combine with your studies or a full-time opportunity to kickstart your career? Let’s get in touch, we’d love to meet you!
json.companyBriefDescription: We are revolutionizing the way people do their groceries. Our delivery service offers our customers a cheaper, easier, faster, and more sustainable alternative.

json.descriptionHtml:

From developing personalized shopping experiences, like our Meal Planner, to building engaging loyalty programs, we focus on delivering a high-quality, intuitive user experience by building scalable solutions with the latest tech. If you're passionate about building impactful products used by millions daily, this is the place for you!

Tricks of the trade

  • Ownership: Take ownership of projects from concept to production. Work collaboratively, grow alongside your team, and contribute to the team's success.
  • Impact: Develop solutions that make shopping smarter, faster, and more intuitive. Your work will power seamless, scalable, and personalized interactions for millions of users.
  • Agility: Thrive in a fast-paced environment where you learn and use a wide variety of tools and platforms to build the best tech for our customers.
  • Amplification: You'll share knowledge, empower your colleagues and multiply your impact beyond what you could achieve on your own.
  • Platform thinking: Build technology that empowers developers and business analysts alike to develop flexible features. (Check out our blog post!)
  • Our Tech: You'll work with Java and TypeScript on a daily basis, using frameworks like Spring (Spring with Spring Boot, Reactor, Immutables) and React. Supporting this core stack are GraalVM, Apache Calcite, Maven, Python 3.x, MongoDB, PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, Kafka, Git, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Helm, Terraform, Vault, and Datadog.

We're looking for

  • You have a Master's Degree (in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Information Technology, Computer Engineering or a related technical field) or equivalent experience.
  • You have 5+ years of professional experience in Java.
  • You have a product mindset and treat technology as a means to an end, owning and solving business problems by choosing the best-fitting technical solutions and tools for each challenge.
  • You are fluent in working with AI and can effectively accelerate your output with orchestrated agentic workflows.
  • Your English skills are on point (no Dutch required).

Picnic Perks

✍ Every expert was once a beginner!

You'll get plenty of opportunities to challenge yourself and grow, including the Picnic Tech Academy, Lunch & Learn sessions, and tailored soft skills training. We also offer free professional weekly language courses.


Make a difference

You'll work in an awesome startup environment with the freedom to drive your own projects and create a visible impact. Our fully electric vehicles and sustainable business model mean you'll also be contributing to making the world a better place!


‍ Teamwork makes the dream work

With more than 80 nationalities across 3 countries, you'll be part of a diverse company with plenty of cool stuff to get involved with, from board game evenings to after-work drinks to our company ski trip and more!


You are what you eat

You'll get freshly prepared, healthy lunches and snacks (with plenty of vegetarian, vegan, and halal options). Coffee snob? Don't worry, our amazing Picnic barista has you covered.


Stay healthy

Mental health is important. As well as having the option to speak with Picnic colleagues who act as confidential advisors, our collaboration with OpenUp gives you easy access to professional psychologists, along with workshops and materials. There are plenty of sports communities and events to get involved with, from tennis to yoga, to climbing!


Attractive package

We offer competitive compensation and a pension plan that looks out for your future self, as well as 25 vacation days per year, so you can recharge your batteries


Benefits for expats

It can be daunting starting a new job AND moving to a new country. That's why we offer lots of support for our many expat colleagues, if you want to find our relocation benefits, see here.

json.companyDescription:
At Picnic, we're transforming grocery shopping without physical stores, making it cheaper, easier, faster, and more sustainable since 2015. With +160 locations across 3 countries, we grow thanks to our passionate people and our iconic electric cars!

Personal and professional growth are our priority. We offer our colleagues individual mentorship with assigned team leads, custom-made development paths on our internal learning platform, language courses, and career opportunities through our internal vacancies website.

Want to join one of our warehouses? You’re welcome without a CV nor previous experience! We offer you free lunch and dinner every day, and a multicultural environment with more than 80 nationalities!
Interested in becoming a delivery driver? You’ll explore your city in your own electric vehicle! We also offer you lots of free snacks and a ping pong table to enjoy your break. On top of that, we encourage all our colleagues to join our legendary Picnic parties!

Are you looking for a side job to combine with your studies or a full-time opportunity to kickstart your career? Let’s get in touch, we’d love to meet you!
json.companyBriefDescription: We are revolutionizing the way people do their groceries. Our delivery service offers our customers a cheaper, easier, faster, and more sustainable alternative.

json.title: Senior Tools Engineer
json.descriptionHtml:
Frankfurt Programming / Engineering CRYENGINE

We are looking for an experienced and talented Senior Tools Engineer for our CRYENGINE Tools team at our new state-of-the-art office in Frankfurt, Germany.

As a Senior Tools Engineer, you will be working in a cooperative, cross-disciplinary environment to develop industry-leading tools, contributing to the improvement of productivity and quality for game teams and the CRYENGINE Sandbox Editor. As part of our innovative and ambitious team, you can expect your abilities and scope of duties to grow as you make a real difference.


What You'll Do
    • Help create and deploy our suite of development tools, both in-cloud and on-prem, across multiple platforms.
    • Contribute to the development of the CRYENGINE Sandbox Editor.
    • Collaborate closely with artists, designers, and software engineers on the requirements and design of tools.
    • Propose tools and methods to increase the efficiency and quality of content development.
    • Contribute to maintenance and improvements of critical low-level systems.
    • Ensure reliability and stability of the code base, and drive adoption of new techniques, technologies and languages.
What You Will Deliver
    • 5+ years of relevant professional software development experience.
    • Excellent C#, C++ programming skills.
    • Experience with distributed, micro-service architectures and software configuration and deployment with Kubernetes.
    • Experience developing user interfaces and working with HTML and Qt frameworks (or similar).
    • Expertise with performance profiling tools.
    • Willing to relocate to Frankfurt am Main area, Germany.
Nice To Have (Not Required)
    • Experience working with multithreaded, real-time systems.
    • Knowledge of WPF and/or TSX (e.g. React)
    • Strong TypeScript, Python, Rust, Go programming skills.
    • Experience with any game Engines preferably experience with CRYENGINE.

What you can expect from us

Career Path
Your professional development is important to us, so we have laid out a career plan to help you progress towards your goals and objectives.
Relocation Support
We offer a relocation budget and full coverage of flights to Frankfurt for you and your family.
You can expect extensive assistance with visa, work permits, and communication with authorities during the relocation process, as well as help settling into Germany (e.g. setting up appointments with banks, government agencies, schools, landlords, finding apartments etc.).
Company Apartment
To help you get settled, we provide you with a fully furnished company apartment during your first three months in Frankfurt.
Public Transport Pass
Discover Frankfurt by bus, tram and metro – free of charge.
Gym Card
A healthy body is a healthy mind. We offer a membership at the premium gym chain Fitness First in Germany. Work out, join group fitness classes, or relax in the wellness facilities.
State-of-the-art Office
We’ve recently moved into a brand-new, modern office located in the heart of Frankfurt. Our new workspace is designed to inspire creativity and collaboration, with open areas, quiet zones, and top-tier facilities — all just steps away from public transport, restaurants, bars and cultural hotspots.
Hybrid Working
We believe in the value of in-person collaboration while also supporting flexibility. Our hybrid working model includes three days per week in the office and the option to work remotely up to two days per week, unless role-specific requirements or business needs require you to be on site.
Equal Opportunity & Inclusion
We’re building a workplace where everyone belongs. We welcome people of all backgrounds and identities and are proud to be a truly global team, with employees from over 42 countries contributing their ideas, cultures, and perspectives every day.
German Classes
Understanding the local culture will make your stay abroad more enjoyable, and Crytek supports that by offering German language courses for you and your family.
Events
Join us on our exciting company events, including new starter breakfasts, summer and winter parties, our annual trip to Gamescom in Cologne, and many more!
Vacation Days
At our Frankfurt office you can enjoy 24 days of vacation per year, and every 2 years you get 1 more (up to a maximum of 28 days). You will also have on average 10 public holidays on top of the days you take off.

Please apply directly online and, if applicable, upload your materials as specified on the job posting.

json.companyDescription: Crytek is a German video game company, founded in 1999 by brothers Cevat, Avni, and Faruk Yerli, that is headquartered in Frankfurt. Crytek has six other studios, in Budapest, Istanbul, Kiev, Seoul, Shanghai, and Sofia.The company is best known for developing Far Cry and the Crysis series, the open world nature of their games which showcase the company's CryEngine, and for pushing the limits on PC specifications to achieve advanced graphics and gameplay.

json.title: Senior Software Engineer
json.descriptionHtml:
At Payconiq we provide a complete Payment Platform for Banks, Merchants and Payment Service providers. Our 150-person team from more than 40 different backgrounds is continuing to modernise existing payment infrastructure with proven, scalable, real-time payment solutions across the entire omnichannel experience.

We at Payconiq live collaboration, transparency, ownership, adaptability to change and achieving excellence. All of us are constantly on the lookout to raise the bar even higher than yesterday. Together we make sure we’re ever-evolving!


To help us achieve our never-ending goal to learn and grow we are now looking for an experienced Senior Software Engineer to join our international team of open-minded, ambitious and fun colleagues. Do you like what we do and what we stand for? Do you want to grow with us and add real value? Apply now and find out what we have to offer you!

Being a Senior Software Engineer at Payconiq means you will contribute to the whole backend software development lifecycle. You will take the lead in determining the software requirements, creating the architecture and system design, writing the code, testing and deployment. While you are bound to one of our feature areas, you will work within a cross-functional team and support other teams where necessary. Your team will be working on different applications within our payment platform such as iDeal. On a day-to-day basis your focus will be on writing the cleanest code while taking existing system designs into consideration and mentoring others. In addition, you will contribute to and take a key role in further improving and building out our software architecture. You are always on the lookout for ways to do things even better and inspire to share your findings with others.


As a Senior Software Engineer you’re able to bring:

  • The desire to take ownership over everything you do.
  • A self-starter mentality, being able to take the lead on backend software projects and architectural designs.
  • A critical view; always looking for ways to further improve your own and our teams efforts.
  • A desire to work with cutting-edge tech.
  • Good communication skills; high proficiency in English and the ability to clearly and concretely express yourself.
  • An analytical mindset with an intrinsic sense of curiosity; being able to see the bigger picture and wanting to understand how things are working at all levels of the system.
  • Positivity and a ‘can-do’ attitude; always looking for ways to create a welcoming environment for colleagues and never shy to take action where needed.
  • Your honesty opinion and an ability to provide constructive feedback to others.
  • Responsibility for our code quality and system designs.
  • A desire to share knowledge and the ability to act as a mentor to others.
  • The ambition to grow with us and have fun while doing so.


In addition, you’re able to bring the following technical expertise:

  • 7+ years of experience as a Backend Software Developer, having worked on various projects and experience working with various coding styles and languages.
  • Experience of software architecture and system design.
  • Ability to write high quality modular, scalable and reusable code; you live TDD & BDD.
  • Extensive knowledge of the backend software development lifecycle.
  • Understanding of Kotlin (considered as an advantage).
  • Solid understanding of Microservices, REST APIs and Event sourcing.
  • Deep understanding of Java, Spring Frameworks, MongoDB and NoSQL.
  • Decent understanding of and experience with CI/CD, AWS, Kubernetes and Docker.
  • Experience working with agile methodologies and best practices.


If you like what you’ve read, feel free to apply and get the conversation started.


Not sold just yet? Have a peek at some of our perks:

  • Salary of EUR 65.000 – 75.000 gross per year
  • 30 vacation days per year with the possibility to purchase up to 10 extra days
  • Commuting allowance covers 0.21€ per km
  • Pension plan (in addition to your base salary, no personal contribution, fully paid by employer)
  • Collective health insurance with CZ (reduced fees)
  • ANW, WIA, collective accident insurance (paid by Payconiq)
  • Training Budget: 5% of annual gross salary (in addition to your base salary)
  • WFH allowance: EUR 2 per worked day from home
  • Internet allowance: 25 euro per month
  • Desk & Chair from employer: employee is owner
  • Mobile phone policy: bring your own device EUR 500 per 2-year, SIM card from Payconiq
  • Structural WFH Policy: hybrid model
  • Voluntary work: 3 days per year
  • MacBook pro laptop
  • LinkedIn Learning
  • ASR Vitality program
  • Relocation package: Visa sponsorship and digital support throughout the relocation process


How the hiring process will look like:

  • 3 interviews with: HR, your team and the Hiring Manager

Do you want to grow with us and add real value? Apply now and find out what we have to offer!

json.title: Systems/Software Test & Integration Engineer
json.descriptionHtml:
Location: Hengelo, Netherlands

About the team
Thales is a multinational company that operates in several industries, including defense, aerospace, transportation, and security. Thales provides advanced technology solutions to customers in these industries to help them meet their operational requirements and improve their overall performance.
Within the Defense Mission System (DMS) sector of our organization, Thales is seeking an experienced Systems Integration and Test Engineer to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for ensuring the successful integration, verification, validation, and qualification of our products and systems. You will work closely with other engineers and stakeholders to ensure that our products meet customer requirements and are ready for deployment.


About your future job

The main tasks are:

  • Develop and execute integration, verification, validation, and qualification plans and procedures for our products and systems

  • Collaborate with other engineers to identify and resolve integration issues.

  • Develop test cases and perform testing to ensure that our products meet customer requirements.

  • Document test results and provide feedback to development teams.

  • Participate in design reviews and provide input on testability and test coverage.

  • Identify areas for process improvement and recommend changes to increase efficiency and effectiveness.

  • Work with project teams to ensure that our products and systems are delivered on time and within budget.

Why you'd be a good match

  • Bachelor's degree in a technical domain (e.g., electrical engineering or software engineering).

  • Minimum of 3 years of experience in integration, verification, validation, and qualification of complex systems.

  • Experience with requirements management tools and software testing tools.

  • Strong team player with excellent communication and collaboration skills.

  • Ability to travel as required to support project needs.

  • Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.

What's in it for you

  • A challenging job in a great team within our high-tech, innovative work environment. Includes the freedom for initiative and good ideas;

  • Room for personal and professional growth within our organisation, for example in regards to technical, commercial or international skills;

  • Valuable international collaboration with coworkers from 68 different countries around the globe;

  • A comfortable work space in a modern environment, with a focus on flexibility and the right work life balance;

  • You can usually work where and when you want to be able to do your work as well and efficiently as possible. Within Thales we implement hybrid working, where you can, for example, work from home two days a week if your role allows it;

  • A good salary, fitting travel allowance, and the ability to personalise your employment conditions: for instance, you can trade days off for internet costs, our bicycle plan, company fitness and relocation costs;

  • 40 days off per year (PTO). Yes, really.

Interested?
Apply now! Click on the button below to upload your profile and show your interest.

#LI-EC1

json.companyDescription:
We are people, empowered. At Thales, we bring together our passion for technology, engineering and innovation to take on today’s exciting challenges and deliver tomorrow’s impactful solutions. 

In a world that is increasingly fast moving and unpredictable — and full of opportunities — customers count on us to help make life better and keep people safer. Across the United States and around the globe, our team applies its strengths in cybersecurity, connectivity, artificial intelligence and big data to collaboratively solve problems and transform the way our customers do business.

Combining a unique diversity of expertise, talents and cultures, our professionals design and deliver extraordinary solutions. As the architects of decisive moments, we’re making the world a smarter, safer, better place. Learn more about who we are and the work that inspires us, and find out what we can do, together.
json.companyBriefDescription: Thales is a global technology leader with more than 81,000 employees across 68 countries, operating in the markets of defence and security, aerospace and space, digital identity and cybersecurity.

json.title: Junior Software Engineer – Mid-Office
json.descriptionHtml:
About BlockTech

BlockTech is a fast-paced algorithmic trading firm facilitating global cryptocurrency derivatives and spot trading while expanding into new markets. As we continue to grow rapidly, we are looking for a Software Engineer to join our Mid-office team amid our exciting scale-up phase!

The role

In this role, you will work on the systems that keep our trading operation accurate at every level, positions, PnL, instruments, hedging, and the data flowing between them. Across exchanges and asset classes, your work determines whether the numbers our traders and risk team rely on are correct.

You will:

  • Own the books. Maintain and evolve our position, trade, and PnL systems so they remain accurate across every venue we operate on.
  • Build the platform that supports trading. Design services that handle real-time exchange flow with the throughput and reliability on which our quoting and hedging depend.
  • Own the data flows. Operate the public and private data pipelines that bring market prices, account state, and trade events into our systems in real time.
  • Integrate new venues. Deliver exchange and prime-broker integrations end-to-end, protocol, account model, settlement, reconciliation, and surveillance.
  • Model the instrument universe. Own the systems that describe every tradable instrument we trade, lifecycle, expiry, margin, RFQs, fast-path quoting, where both correctness and latency matter.
  • Develop self-healing systems. Build the detection and repair flows that identify position discrepancies and correct them automatically.
  • Build hedging infrastructure. Design and refine the gamma and delta hedging systems that our trading strategies depend on.
  • Deliver reporting. Provide the data that internal stakeholders and regulators require, PnL breakdowns, trade surveillance, treasury accounting, and compliance.

What's in it for you?

  • A full-time position in a relaxed and modern office in Amsterdam
  • Competitive compensation consisting of a base salary combined with a very attractive bonus plan based on individual and company performance
  • Outstanding performance is rewarded with the opportunity to buy into the trading fund
  • Pension plan, company laptop, and reimbursement of your internet costs at home
  • An extensive in-house training program and an annual learning & development budget
  • The opportunity to work at the forefront of automated trading using state-of-the-art technology, in an environment that embraces AI to both accelerate productivity and advance our systems and models
  • Gym membership reimbursement and the opportunity to engage in various sports during working hours, including kickboxing, CrossFit, fitness, and soccer
  • Regular social events, including weekly Friday drinks, monthly outings, quarterly sports competitions and bi-annual trips abroad
  • Daily breakfast and warm lunch. Additionally, snacks and drinks are provided throughout the day
  • For internationals: All-in relocation package and Dutch classes

Our culture

At our core, we are a team of passionate trading and tech enthusiasts committed to revolutionizing trading through automation. Our collaborative approach ensures that everyone contributes to achieving our ambitious goals.

In this role, you will be both challenged and stretched, with ample opportunities for growth and development, both within and beyond your main responsibilities.

We invest in our people by offering competitive compensation and benefits, fostering innovation, and celebrating success. Our commitment to personal development transcends traditional norms; we provide an equal playing field, promote reverse leadership, and offer boundless growth opportunities for all our employees.

Join us to unlock your potential and drive innovation in the exciting world of algorithmic trading.


  • 1+ years of commercial working experience as a Software Engineer. We are looking for an engineer with breadth: someone comfortable moving between a Rust service, a Python data pipeline, and a complex production issue, and choosing the right tool for each.
  • Familiarity with Grafana & PostgreSQL
  • A critical mind that follows things until completion
  • The ability to confidently work on completely new components
  • A high level of responsibility and ownership
  • Good written and verbal communication skills in English

Short paragraph: As a Software Engineer in our Mid-office team, you’ll design, build, and maintain high-performance systems that power global crypto trading. From scalable data solutions to real-time monitoring and compliance reporting, you’ll own projects end-to-end, collaborating closely with trading and operations to drive efficiency, accuracy, and innovation.

Location: Amsterdam

Department: Tech
